commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a per-session retained compaction checkpoint byte budget with a configurable `session.maintenance.compactionCheckpointMaxBytes` setting, plus schema, docs, and filesystem-backed regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +120, Tests +197, Docs +1. Total +318 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection of current main shows checkpoint retention is count-only, and the PR's filesystem-backed tests describe a clear current-main failure path with oversized retained checkpoint files.
commands.review_followup: No automated repair is needed now; the remaining action is maintainer review of the protected PR's new config/default retention contract.

target.title: fix(gateway): cap retained compaction checkpoint bytes
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: compaction checkpoint retention was count-only, so repeated compactions could retain many large full-transcript checkpoint snapshots for one session.
- Solution: keep the existing count cap and add a retained checkpoint byte budget per session, trimming older checkpoint metadata and files when either limit is exceeded.
- What changed: `persistSessionCompactionCheckpoint` now stats retained checkpoint snapshots before trimming and applies both count and byte limits. The default retained-byte budget is 128 MiB per session and can be tuned with `session.maintenance.compactionCheckpointMaxBytes`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no checkpoint file format changes, no protocol changes, and no broad gateway scheduler/backpressure changes.

## Motivation

- The live performance investigation found many retained `.checkpoint.<uuid>.jsonl` artifacts around 14-16 MB each in a single active agent session directory. Count-only retention allowed this to grow far beyond the amount of checkpoint data needed for recovery.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #84822
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: repeated compaction checkpoints now retain by total snapshot bytes per session instead of count only.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw worktree on Ubuntu/WSL2 with Node 24.15.0; regression uses real temp session-store JSON and real sparse checkpoint files on disk.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/session-compaction-checkpoints.test.ts src/config/zod-schema.session-maintenance-extensions.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/gateway/session-compaction-checkpoints.ts src/gateway/session-compaction-checkpoints.test.ts src/config/zod-schema.session.ts src/config/zod-schema.session-maintenance-extensions.test.ts src/config/schema.help.ts src/config/schema.labels.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts src/config/types.base.ts`
  - `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`
  - `pnpm docs:list`
- Evidence after fix: terminal capture from the focused test run:

```text
RUN  v4.1.6 /home/galini/GitHub/worktrees/bug-026-033-gateway-perf

Test Files  5 passed (5)
     Tests  54 passed (54)
  Start at  20:25:03
  Duration  11.75s (transform 4.80s, setup 846ms, import 10.10s, tests 476ms, environment 1ms)
```

- Observed result after fix: the byte-budget test creates eight old checkpoint files plus a current checkpoint file sized to exceed the default 128 MiB retained-byte budget, persists a checkpoint, verifies the three oldest checkpoint files are deleted, verifies the newest old checkpoint files and current checkpoint remain, and verifies stored metadata keeps only the retained checkpoints. A second regression test sets `session.maintenance.compactionCheckpointMaxBytes` to a tight budget and verifies the override drives older checkpoint eviction while keeping the newest checkpoint.
- What was not tested: a live long-running gateway compaction cycle was not rerun against a private production-like agent session; the proof is a filesystem-backed gateway checkpoint seam test plus config/schema coverage and redacted before evidence from the live sample.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): redacted live sample showed many retained checkpoint files around 14-16 MB each, with earlier same-investigation `pidstat` read I/O averaging `kB_rd/s=40768.06` over a 5s sample.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: compaction checkpoint retention used only `MAX_COMPACTION_CHECKPOINTS_PER_SESSION = 25`, while each retained snapshot could be up to 64 MiB.
- Missing detection / guardrail: tests covered count trimming but not retained snapshot bytes or the configurable retained-byte budget.
- Contributing context (if known): memory-host session scanning already skips checkpoint artifacts, so the remaining pressure was the retained checkpoint artifact set itself.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/gateway/session-compaction-checkpoints.test.ts`, `src/config/zod-schema.session-maintenance-extensions.test.ts`, `src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: persisting a checkpoint trims older checkpoint metadata and checkpoint files when retained snapshot bytes exceed the per-session budget, and `session.maintenance.compactionCheckpointMaxBytes` can tune that budget with schema/help coverage.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: checkpoint persistence owns the metadata and file cleanup decision, and the test uses real files without requiring a live gateway session. Config/schema tests prove the new operator-facing setting is accepted, documented, and rejects invalid size strings.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing count-trim coverage only.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Long-running sessions retain fewer old compaction checkpoint snapshots when their checkpoint artifacts exceed the retained-byte budget. The default budget is 128 MiB per session and operators can override it with `session.maintenance.compactionCheckpointMaxBytes`, for example `"256mb"` or `"33554432b"`.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[repeated compactions] -> [up to 25 full checkpoint snapshots retained]

After:
[repeated compactions] -> [newest checkpoints retained within count + byte budget] -> [older checkpoint files removed]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Ubuntu/WSL2
- Runtime/container: Node 24.15.0, local worktree
- Model/provider: N/A for checkpoint retention
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): temp session store in the regression test; override coverage uses `session.maintenance.compactionCheckpointMaxBytes` with a small byte budget.

### Steps

1. Create a session store with retained checkpoint metadata entries pointing at real checkpoint files.
2. Size each checkpoint file so the existing checkpoints plus the new checkpoint exceed the default per-session retained-byte budget.
3. Persist a new compaction checkpoint.
4. Repeat with `session.maintenance.compactionCheckpointMaxBytes` set to a tighter budget.

### Expected

Older checkpoint metadata and files are trimmed until retained checkpoint bytes fit the configured budget while the newest checkpoint remains available.

### Actual

After this patch, the regression tests verify the oldest checkpoint files are deleted, the newest checkpoint remains, stored metadata only keeps retained checkpoints, and the config override changes the retained-byte budget.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: default byte-budget checkpoint trimming with real temp files; configurable byte-budget trimming; invalid config size rejection; schema help/label coverage; existing checkpoint capture/fork/persist coverage still passes.
- Edge cases checked: current checkpoint remains retained even when byte trimming removes older checkpoints; count-trim behavior still passes; invalid `compactionCheckpointMaxBytes` values fail schema validation.
- What you did **not** verify: live private-session repeated compaction under gateway load.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: Existing configs keep the 128 MiB default. Operators who want a different retained-checkpoint budget can set `session.maintenance.compactionCheckpointMaxBytes` to a byte-size value such as `"64mb"`, `"128mb"`, or `"256mb"`.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: older checkpoints become unavailable sooner for very large sessions.
  - Mitigation: the newest checkpoint is always retained, the existing count cap remains, the default 128 MiB budget still allows multiple 14-16 MB checkpoints from the observed sample, and operators can raise or lower the budget with `session.maintenance.compactionCheckpointMaxBytes`.
- Risk: adding a config surface can drift from docs/help/schema.
  - Mitigation: the PR updates the exported config type, Zod schema, help text, labels, docs, and focused config tests.
